name: State of matter — liquid properties
question_text: Which state of matter has a definite volume but takes the shape of its container?
general_feedback: Liquids have a fixed volume because intermolecular forces are strong enough to keep molecules close, but the molecules can flow past each other, so a liquid adopts the shape of its container.
correct_feedback: Correct — Liquids have a fixed volume and flow to fill the container's shape.

answers.feedback: Liquids have a fixed volume and flow to fill the container's shape.
A gas has neither fixed volume nor fixed shape — it expands to fill its container.
A solid has both fixed volume and fixed shape.
A plasma, like a gas, has no fixed volume or shape.
